🟣 Stroke-Adapted Version: Includes lesion-dependent network testing, lateralized motor/language assessment, and post-stroke phenotype mapping

🟣 FNON NOTE: Stroke Relevance: Post-stroke DMN disruption depends on lesion location. Cortical strokes cause focal DMN HYPO with self-awareness deficits, neglect, or anosognosia. Subcortical white matter strokes cause diffuse DMN HYPO via disconnection, manifesting as flat affect and reduced self-referential processing.

title_block: Stage 4 — Patient Journey | Evidence-Based Neurological Examination

This checklist guides the Fellow through a structured neurological examination for Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI). The focus is on standardised scoring, phenotype identification, and selection of appropriate validated assessment instruments.
title: SOZO BRAIN CENTER — CYPRUS

title_block: 🟣 FNON + Evidence-Based Assessment — Includes Brain Network Analysis
Scoring Key: HYPO (1)  |  NORMAL (0)  |  HYPER (2)          Total: 44 Tests (Max Score: 88)
🟣 TBI-Adapted Version: Includes post-concussion symptom markers, white matter disconnection tests, and TBI severity phenotype indicators

🟣 FNON NOTE: TBI Relevance: TBI causes DMN HYPO through white matter disconnection — axonal shearing disrupts PCC-mPFC connectivity underlying self-referential processing. Chronic TBI shows DMN failure with poor autobiographical integration, emotional numbing, and identity disruption.

title_block: SOZO Response Assessment, Methylphenidate/Amantadine-tDCS Documentation & FNON Non-Responder Pathway
FNON + Evidence-Based Assessment — Includes Brain Network Analysis
Define response at end of block (typically 10–20 sessions). Patient must meet criteria in at least one domain.
This avoids "false non-responder" labelling due to post-exertional symptom worsening and cognitive fatigue on testing day.
title: SOZO BRAIN CENTER — PARTNERS TIER

sections.title: PATIENT CLASSIFICATION: □ Responder  □ Partial Responder  □ Non-Responder
DO NOT escalate intensity beyond safety limits. Always follow SOZO FNON Protocol safety parameters.

title_block: Stage 4 — Patient Journey | Evidence-Based Neurological Examination

This checklist guides the Fellow through a structured neurological examination for Tinnitus. The focus is on standardised scoring, phenotype identification, and selection of appropriate validated assessment instruments.
title: SOZO BRAIN CENTER — CYPRUS

title_block: 🟣 FNON + Evidence-Based Assessment — Includes Brain Network Analysis
Scoring Key: HYPO (1)  |  NORMAL (0)  |  HYPER (2)          Total: 44 Tests (Max Score: 88)
🟣 Tinnitus-Adapted Version: Includes tinnitus-related distress markers, auditory salience network tests, and Tinnitus network phenotype indicators

🟣 FNON NOTE: Tinnitus Relevance: Tinnitus chronifies through DMN HYPERACTIVITY: the phantom sound becomes incorporated into self-referential thought, leading to tinnitus-related rumination, identity disruption ('my life is ruined by this sound'), and persistent default mode engagement with tinnitus as the signal.
title: SOZO BRAIN CENTER — PARTNERS TIER
